headline: Treasuries Extend Slump as Likelihood of Fed Rate Cuts Fades
summary: Rate traders are now pricing in a higher chance of a Fed rate hike this year than they are a rate cut.

raw.headline: Treasuries Extend Slump as Likelihood of Fed Rate Cuts Fades
raw.summary: Rate traders are now pricing in a higher chance of a Fed rate hike this year than they are a rate cut.

headline: Bond Yields Extend Surge, Traders See Chance of Rate Hikes This Year
summary: Could the Federal Reserve actually need to raise interest rates later this year?  Rates traders are now considering that possibility, assigning a more than 40% chance of at least one rate hike by October on Friday morning, up from just 6% a day earlier, according to CME Group data.  Government bonds extended their selloff on Friday, with the yield on the 10-year Treasury recently hitting 4.38%, according to Tradeweb, up from 4.281% a day earlier.
